task_completion.description: Navigate to Denver International Airport (DEN) or the specified coordinates in a GIS application like Google Earth.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIS application (e.g., Google Earth) displays Denver International Airport or the coordinates 39.8279°N, 104.6647°W.

task_completion.description: Use a ruler or measurement tool to measure the heading of Runway 34L.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A measurement line or ruler tool is visible on the screen, extending from the southern threshold of the runway toward the northern end.

task_completion.description: Identify the heading value from the measurement tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box or status bar shows a heading/bearing value, typically between 340° and 360° (official magnetic heading is 353°).

task_completion.description: Create a new placemark at the southern threshold of Runway 34L.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new placemark icon appears at the southern end of the runway (near 39.8279°N, 104.6647°W as per task instructions).

task_completion.description: Set the placemark name to 'DEN Runway 34L Threshold' and include the measured heading in the description.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The placemark properties window shows Name: 'DEN Runway 34L Threshold' and Description: 'Heading: XXX°' (where XXX is the measured value).

task_completion.description: Save or export the placemark as a KML file to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog shows the file being saved to /home/ga/Documents/runway_34L_heading.kml.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIS software's measurement tools rather than manually calculating or guessing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the ruler/line tool active and aligned with the physical runway on the map.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor or terminal to manually create the KML file without using the GIS tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen interacting with the GIS software's 'Save' or 'Export' features rather than a code editor.

integrity.description: The placemark is placed at the actual runway threshold visible on the map, not random coordinates.
integrity.visual_evidence: The placemark icon is visually positioned at the start of the runway markings for 34L.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The airport is Denver International (DEN). The target runway is 34L. The official magnetic heading for Runway 34L is 353 degrees. The agent is instructed to use the southern threshold (approx 39.8279°N, 104.6647°W per task description, though PI notes 39.8518868, -104.6965353 for the actual 34L threshold). The final output must be a KML file at /home/ga/Documents/runway_34L_heading.kml containing the heading in the description.
